query: You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository. The codebase is at version 2.186 (commit: 752d19b3). ## Issue to Resolve [BUG]: Can't exit previewer through Android back button ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? - Open the Browser - Open the menu and select Preview - Click the Android back button (the one highlighted by yellow in the screenshot) ### Expected behaviour The previewer is closed. ### Actual behaviour Nothing happens on clicking the back button. ### Debug info ```text 2.18 alpha 6 ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share? The previewer can be closed by clicking the back button at the top left. ### Research - [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app) - [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [X] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/SingleFragmentActivity.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/previewer/PreviewerActivity.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality ## Important Evaluation Notes **Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing. **If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files ## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS **NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.
